title: GitLab CI : ApplyLayer stdout:  stderr: remount /, flags: 0x44000: permission denied exit status 1
body:
Hello,

I'm trying to make a pipeline that builds and pushes an image to my container registry on Gitlab. However, I'm getting an error in step 1 when pulling the image of the Dockerfile.

```bash
$ buildah build -t $IMAGE_NAME <directory-with-dockerfile>
STEP 1/8: FROM debian:latest
Resolved "debian" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/debian:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob xxxxx
time="2024-12-05T13:46:08Z" level=error msg="While applying layer: ApplyLayer stdout:  stderr: remount /, flags: 0x44000: permission denied exit status 1"
Error: creating build container: copying system image from manifest list: writing blob: adding layer with blob "xxxxx": ApplyLayer stdout:  stderr: remount /, flags: 0x44000: permission denied exit status 1
```

Here is the Dockerfile I'm trying to build and push :

```bash
default:
  image: quay.io/buildah/stable
  before_script:
    - echo "$CI_REGISTRY_PASSWORD" | buildah login -u "$CI_REGISTRY_USER" --password-stdin $CI_REGISTRY

build-push-image:
  tags:
    - runner0          # Docker executor
  stage: build
  variables:
    STORAGE_DRIVER: vfs
    BUILDAH_FORMAT: docker
    BUILDAH_ISOLATION: chroot
    IMAGE_NAME: "$CI_REGISTRY/<namespace>/<project>/<tag>"
  script:
    - buildah build -t $IMAGE_NAME <directory-with-dockerfile>
    - buildah push $IMAGE_NAME
```

comments.body:
I finally found the culprit : `apparmor`.

For anyone having the same problem, in your gitlab runner, add the following :
```bash
security_opt = [‘apparmor:unconfined’]
```
